checks.name: runs_in_biome arc locked=0 unlocked=3+
checks.reason: save_data={} (get_save_data() now implemented but was missing) → biome_runs_map={} → previous_runs=0 always. Arc using runs_in_biome condition was always locked regardless of actual run count. FIX: get_save_data() implemented this cycle.

checks.name: fins_vues arc locked=0 unlocked=5
checks.reason: same root cause: save_data={} → fins_vues=0 always. FIX: get_save_data() implemented this cycle unblocks.

checks.name: oghams_owned locked=1 unlocked=2
checks.reason: save_data={} → oghams={} → owned=[].size()=0. FIX: get_save_data() implemented this cycle unblocks.

checks.name: faction_rep arc evaluation correct
checks.reason: llm_adapter_map_prompt.gd:97 defaults arc_actual_value=rep_value from context.faction_rep. faction_rep sourced from game_state via _build_context → correct. Default 20 < arc_condition_value(30) = locked. Growing rep unlocks arc.

checks.name: biome_runs_map uses 'biome_runs' key
checks.reason: merlin_skeleton_generator.gd:49 uses save_data.get('biome_runs', {}) — correct key. Previous bug 'biome_history' was fixed in S2-ARC-CONDITION-EVAL cycle.

checks.name: context dict has fins_vues and oghams_owned keys
checks.reason: merlin_skeleton_generator.gd:74-75 correctly adds fins_vues and oghams_owned to context dict. llm_adapter_map_prompt.gd:100-103 reads them correctly.

root_cause: S2-ARC-CONDITION-EVAL fixed the arc_condition_type dispatch but did NOT implement get_save_data() in MerlinStore. The dispatch code is correct but save_data={} fallback meant all save-sourced values were 0.
fix_applied: get_save_data() implemented in merlin_store.gd:649 — calls save_system.load_profile() to return disk-synced profile with biome_runs, fins_vues, oghams.

invariants_after_fix.runs_in_biome_0: biome_runs_map.get(biome_id,0)=0 → arc locked when no previous runs in biome
invariants_after_fix.faction_rep_correct: rep from game state, not corrupted by save_data gap

regression_risk: NONE — save_system.load_profile() already used in _ready() for meta loading. Adding it to get_save_data() adds one extra disk read per biome skeleton generation (acceptable, director-approved).
